name: Kong Linghui
nameEn: KONG LINGHUI 路 Table Tennis Prince 路 Grand Slam

tags.text: 馃弳 World Cup Singles Champion

timeline.subtitle: First World Cup Title
achievements: 馃弳 Men's World Cup Singles Champion (first major singles title)

timeline.subtitle: World Championships Triumph 路 Breakthrough
achievements: 馃弳 Tianjin World Championships Men's Singles Champion (beat Jean-Philippe Gatien in final)
馃弳 Mixed Doubles Champion (with Deng Yaping)
馃搶 Iconic figure in Chinese men's table tennis resurgence

timeline.subtitle: Atlanta Olympic Double
achievements: 馃 Atlanta Olympics Men's Doubles Gold (with Liu Guoliang)
馃 Atlanta Olympics Men's Singles Runner-up

timeline.subtitle: World Championships Doubles
achievements: 馃弳 Manchester World Championships Men's Doubles Champion (with Liu Guoliang)

timeline.subtitle: Defending World Doubles
achievements: 馃弳 Eindhoven World Championships Men's Doubles Champion (with Liu Guoliang, successful defense)

timeline.subtitle: Sydney Triumph 路 Grand Slam
achievements: 馃 Sydney Olympics Men's Singles Gold (beat Jan-Ove Waldner 4:3 in final)
馃搶 Completed Grand Slam with Olympic, World and World Cup singles titles
馃搶 Second male Grand Slam champion in Chinese history

timeline.subtitle: World Doubles Again
achievements: 馃弳 Osaka World Championships Men's Doubles Champion (with Liu Guoliang, three-peat)

timeline.subtitle: Athens Olympics
achievements: 馃搶 Athens Olympics Men's Doubles (with Wang Hao) eliminated in quarterfinals
馃弳 Multiple ITTF Pro Tour titles

timeline.subtitle: Retirement
achievements: 馃搶 Later became coach, served as head coach of Chinese women's team

achievements.title: 猸?Grand Slam
achievements.description: Kong Linghui is the second male Grand Slam champion in Chinese history, winning singles titles at the Olympics (2000), World Championships (1995), and World Cup (1994). Together with Liu Guoliang, they were known as the 'Twin Stars' and ushered in the era of Chinese men's table tennis dominance.
